name: Pasta Primavera with Roasted Red Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chopped fresh parsley (preferably Italian flat-leaf)
recipeInstructions:
(From "Mediterranean Light" by Martha Rose Shulman, Bantam).
Roast 2 of the red peppers under a broiler or above a burner flame, turning until all sides are charred. Remove from heat and place in a paper bag or damp towel. Allow to cool.
Meanwhile, cut off the tips of the asparagus, about 2 inches from the top. Trim off the tough base and cut remaining stalks into pieces about 1/2 inch long. Keep tops separate.
Cut remaining red pepper into thin strips, then halve or third them.
When roasted peppers are cool enough to handle, remove skins and pat dry. Remove stems, seeds, and membranes, cutting into thin strips. Place in a bowl with 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1 garlic clove, coarse salt, and basil leaves cut into slivers. Toss together, cover, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Mince remaining basil. Steam peas, asparagus tips, and stalks separately until crisp-tender (5 minutes for asparagus, 5-10 minutes for peas). Refresh under cold water.
Boil a large pot of salted water. Cook pasta al dente, then drain and toss with remaining olive oil and garlic clove. Refrigerate or serve at room temperature.
Before serving, toss pasta with steamed peas, asparagus stalks, sliced raw pepper strips, minced basil, parsley, Parmesan, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve on plates topped with spoonful of roasted peppers. Place spears of asparagus and a few radishes on the side.
